articles.headline: Looking for starting RB money
articles.story: Allgeier is seeking a contract valued at around $22 million over three years,  Josh Kendall of The Athletic  reports.
articles.analysis: Allgeier set the Falcons' rookie rushing record with 1,035 yards on 210 carries across 16 regular-season games in 2022 but spent the next three years as Atlanta's top backup behind Bijan Robinson. Allgeier was still productive in an RB2 role and rushed for eight touchdowns over 17 regular-season games in 2025, but with the Falcons set to spend significant capital on an extension for Robinson this offseason, it appears as though the former will be suiting up for a different team in 2026.

articles.headline: Leading receiver in wild-card loss
articles.story: Smith caught eight of 11 targets for 70 yards in Sunday's 23-19 wild-card game loss to the 49ers.
articles.analysis: Smith accounted for 70 of  Jalen Hurts ' 168 passing yards while no teammate had more than 33 receiving yards. Sunday's playoff disappointment comes on the heels of Smith's third 1,000-yard regular season in five NFL campaigns, as he caught 77 of 113 targets for 1,008 yards and a career-low four touchdowns while dressing for all 17 regular-season games. Smith is tied to the Eagles through the 2028 season on a three-year, $75 million extension signed in April of 2024.

articles.headline: Gets new position mate Tuesday
articles.story: Smith is now joined in the Eagles' wide-receiver room by veteran Hollywood Brown, who signed a one-year deal with Philadelphia on Tuesday, per  Tim McManus of ESPN.com .
articles.analysis: Smith remains firmly entrenched as the Eagles' No. 2 receiver -- and he'd bump up to No. 1 should A.J. Brown be traded -- but the addition of a fellow speedster in Brown may be to Smith's benefit. Philadelphia did have a similarly fleet-footed option as the No. 3 receiver the last two seasons in Jahan Dotson, but he logged just 69 targets across 34 regular-season games in that span. However, Brown could be the recipient of much more consistent volume given both the change at offensive coordinator and his more established track record.

articles.headline: Two catches in victory
articles.story: Samuel caught two of five targets for 20 yards and rushed twice for one yard in Sunday's 24-17 win over the Eagles.
articles.analysis: Samuel was held to two catches for the second straight week, while his 20 receiving yards were his fewest since Week 8. The wide receiver completed his regular season having caught 72 of 99 targets for 727 yards and five touchdowns while also turning 17 carries into 75 yards and a score over 16 contests. Samuel will enter the offseason as a free agent, and he proved he's still got plenty left in the tank, recording his most receptions since the 2021 campaign.

articles.headline: Could be fit with Panthers
articles.story: Nick Shook of NFL.com  suggests Samuel would be a prudent free-agent addition for the Panthers.
articles.analysis: Bryce Young took another step forward in his second year in head coach Dave Canales' offense in 2025, with Carolina's addition of Tetairoa McMillan in the first round of last year's draft proving critical to the quarterback's ascension. Shook sees the potential infusion of Samuel as one that would provide Young with a reliable veteran target alongside McMillan, Jalen Coker and recent signee John Metchie, although he notes Samuel's salary requirements may be a stumbling block.

articles.headline: Links up with Eagles
articles.story: Brown is slated to sign a one-year contract with Philadelphia,  NFL reporter Jordan Schultz  reports.
articles.analysis: With trade speculation still surrounding  A.J. Brown , the addition of the 2019 first-round pick provides some needed depth at wideout for the Eagles behind  DeVonta Smith . Across 16 regular-season appearances with Kansas City in 2025, Brown secured 49 of 74 targets for 587 yards and five scores.

articles.headline: Eagles add competition Friday
articles.story: Brown, who signed with the Eagles in March and is thought to have the inside track to the No. 3 receiver job, gained competition for the role Friday in the form of trade acquisition Dontayvion Wicks,  Rob Demovsky and Adam Schefter of ESPN  report.
articles.analysis: Brown certainly has the more accomplished resume between him and Wicks, but the latter is four years younger and the Eagles saw fit to invest two draft picks to acquire him from the Packers before also signing him to a one-year, $12.5 million extension. Wicks did have trouble with drops over his first three seasons in Green Bay, while Brown has greatly improved in that area compared to earlier in his career, but it's conceivable Wicks makes a strong play for Brown's snaps in training camp and preseason.

articles.headline: Scores lone offensive touchdown
articles.story: Barner caught all four targets for 54 yards and a touchdown in Sunday's 29-13 win over the Patriots in the Super Bowl.
articles.analysis: All four of Barner's catches went for first downs, and he finished second on the team in receiving yards behind  Cooper Kupp  (61). Barner's first postseason touchdown -- a 16-yard reception where he was wide open -- essentially put the game away for the Seahawks in the fourth quarter. The Seahawks trusted the second-year tight end with blocking and pass-catching duties all season, and he finished with 58 receptions for 586 yards and seven touchdowns across 20 games, including the postseason. He should be the leader for the No. 1 TE role in 2026 despite the addition of  Elijah Arroyo  -- a second-round pick in 2025.

articles.headline: Larger role with Fant gone?
articles.story: Barner could be a more significant piece of Seattle's offense moving forward, as  Adam Schefter of ESPN  reports that the team released Noah Fant on Sunday.
articles.analysis: Fant had 500 receiving yards to Barner's 245 in 2024, though Barner scored four touchdowns to Fant's one. Barner and rookie second-round pick Elijah Arroyo are expected to compete for the top spot on the tight end depth chart now that Fant's out of the picture. The Seahawks selected Barner in the fourth round of the 2024 NFL Draft.

articles.headline: Officially re-signs with Chiefs
articles.story: Kelce signed a three-year, $54.74 million contract with Kansas City on Monday,  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: Kelce agreed to terms on a new contract with the Chiefs in early March and now officially re-ups with Kansas City, where he has spent his entire career since being selected by the team in the third round of the 2013 NFL Draft. The 36-year-old tight end has seen his production dip in recent years but nonetheless remains productive, having surpassed 800 receiving yards in both 2024 and 2025. His contract had previously been reported by  Adam Schefter of ESPN  as a one-year, $12 million deal worth up to $15 million, but Kelce and the Chiefs have instead negotiated a multi-year pact. As such, as long as Kelce opts to continue his playing career, he will be positioned to operate as a key cog on offense for QB  Patrick Mahomes  (knee).

articles.headline: Re-signing with Chiefs
articles.story: Kelce has agreed to terms on a new deal with the Chiefs,  Adam Schefter of ESPN  reports.

articles.story: Kelce appears set to return for the 2026 campaign, and the Chiefs are in the mix to re-sign him,  Dianna Russini of The Athletic  reports.

articles.story: The Chiefs have remained in touch with Kelce during the offseason and are hopeful to re-sign him for the 2026 season,  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: Subscribe now  to instantly reveal our take on this news.

articles.headline: Hits more milestones
articles.story: Kelce recorded three receptions on six targets for 12 yards in Sunday's 14-12 loss to the Raiders.

articles.headline: Excited for reunion with former OC
articles.story: Kelce has yet to give an official answer on whether he'll continue playing in 2026, but on a recent episode of his New Heights podcast he seemed very excited about the possibility of working with offensive coordinator Eric Bieniemy again,  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network  reports. "I can't wait to see him back in the building, man," Kelce said. "He's one of my favorite coaches of all time, one of my favorite people of all time. I've had so many unbelievable growing moments under him as a player, as a person, and I just love the guy."
articles.analysis: Bieniemy was Kansas City's OC from 2018 through 2022, and his final year with the team was the last time Kelce topped 1,000 receiving yards in a season. Bieniemy was brought back this offseason to replace Matt Nagy, giving Kelce another reason to run it back. Talks between the veteran tight end, who is a free agent this offseason, and the KC front office are expected to take place soon.

articles.headline: Reworks deal with Philadelphia
articles.story: Elliott agreed Thursday to rework his contract with the Eagles, accepting a $1 million pay cut while receiving $5 million guaranteed for 2026,  Dave Zangaro of NBC Sports Philadelphia  reports.
articles.analysis: The Eagles will save $243,000 in cap space with the maneuver, while the guaranteed money virtually assures that Elliott will remain the team's kicker in 2026 following a rough 2025 campaign. After converting 20 of 27 field-goal attempts for a career-worst 74.1 percent rate during the Eagles' 17 regular-season games, Elliott then had a costly missed extra point in Philadelphia's narrow loss to San Francisco in the wild-card round. The 31-year-old still remains under contract through 2028, but the Eagles would likely have more flexibility to move on from him next offseason if he's unable to bounce back in 2026.

articles.story: Elliott made field goals from 41 and 33 yards in Sunday's 23-19 wild-card loss to the 49ers but missed one of his two extra-point attempts.

articles.headline: Week 5 absence expected
articles.story: Elliott is expected to sit out Sunday's Week 5 clash against the Cardinals due to an ankle injury,  Tim McManus of ESPN.com  reports.
articles.analysis: Elliott's injury occurred late in the third quarter of the Week 4 win over the Jaguars, when he was run into by cornerback Tyson Campbell on a field-goal attempt and drew a roughing the kicker penalty. Philadelphia is expected to roll with University of Texas product Cameron Dicker, who's currently on the practice squad, as the placekicker against Arizona.

articles.headline: Restructures contract
articles.story: Allen (foot) is restructuring his contract with the Bills, creating $12 million of 2026 cap space for the team,  Adam Schefter of ESPN  reports.
articles.analysis: This helps to make room for new addition  DJ Moore , who finished the 2025 regular season with a career-low 682 receiving yards. Moore turns 29 in April and should have more to offer in 2026, joining a Buffalo offense where RB  James Cook , WR  Khalil Shakir  and TE  Dalton Kincaid  are the main weapons. Allen, meanwhile, is recovering from late-January surgery to address a broken bone in his right foot. He should be fine for training camp, if not spring practices, with initial reports estimating a recovery timeline of 8-to-10 weeks.

articles.headline: Facing 8-to-10-week recovery
articles.story: Allen is facing a recovery timetable between eight and 10 weeks after undergoing surgery to address a broken bone in his right foot, which puts him on track for the start of OTAs,  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network  reports.

articles.headline: Team trading for DJ Moore
articles.story: Allen is set to have a new key target in 2026, as  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network  reports that the Bills agreed to trade for DJ Moore on Thursday.
articles.analysis: Moore's 608 receptions, 8,213 receiving yards and 392 receiving first downs in the regular season since being drafted in 2018 all rank sixth in the NFL over that span. The Bills had 2,107 receiving yards by wide receivers in the 2025 regular season, which was ninth-fewest in the NFL. Moore's coming off a down year in which he posted a career-low 682 regular-season receiving yards, but he still figures to work as the top receiving option for Allen once the star quarterback recovers from a broken foot.

articles.headline: Wins first MVP Award
articles.story: Stafford was  named   the 2025 Most Valuable Player on Thursday.
articles.analysis: As expected, Stafford's primary competition for the award was  Drake Maye , with only one first-place vote separating the players. It was Stafford's first MVP award, which was well earned after he recorded a career-best 46 touchdowns while turning the ball over only 11 times across 17 regular-season games. While his future was uncertain after being eliminated in the NFC Championship Game by Seattle, Stafford indicated that he will return for the 2026 season during his acceptance speech, per  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network .

articles.headline: Taking time to weigh playing future
articles.story: Rams head coach Sean McVay said Monday that Stafford will take time this offseason to decide whether to return for the 2026 campaign, but the team is "hopeful" the veteran quarterback will decide to play an 18th season in the NFL,  Sarah Barshop of ESPN.com  reports.
articles.analysis: Subscribe now  to instantly reveal our take on this news.

articles.headline: Masterful performance in NFCCG loss
articles.story: Stafford completed 22 of 35 pass attempts for 374 yards and three touchdowns while adding two carries for 16 yards in Sunday's 31-27 loss to the Seahawks in the NFC Championship Game.
articles.analysis: Subscribe now  to instantly reveal our take on this news.

articles.headline: Throws for over 250 yards in OT win
articles.story: Stafford completed 20 of 42 pass attempts for 258 yards with no touchdowns or interceptions while losing one yard on a kneel-down in Sunday's 20-17 overtime playoff win against Chicago.

articles.headline: Will play through finger sprain

articles.headline: Snags NFL MVP honors
articles.story: Stafford was named the 2025 NFL MVP at Thursday's NFL Honors,  John Breech of CBS Sports  reports.
articles.analysis: The process was anything but dull this year, as Stafford edged out the Patriots' Drake Maye by a single first-place vote thanks to Josh Allen and Justin Herbert garnering three first-place votes between them. Stafford, who turns 38 on Saturday, snagged the award for the first time in his long career after throwing for 4,707 yards with a career-high 46 touchdowns across 17 regular-season games.

articles.headline: Team adds Gainwell to backfield
articles.story: Irving could face increased competition for touches, as  NFL reporter Jordan Schultz  reports Monday that  Kenneth Gainwell  is signing a two-year, $14 million contract with the Buccaneers.
articles.analysis: Irving missed seven games due to foot and shoulder injuries in 2025, and he ceded touches to  Sean Tucker  and  Rachaad White  after returning.  While Irving ultimately led the Buccaneers with 173 rushing attempts, he scored only one rushing touchdown compared to White's four on 132 carries and Tucker's seven on 86 carries. White and Tucker are both free agents, but the addition of Gainwell suggests Irving could be ticketed for less of a workhorse role in 2026, as Gainwell racked up 1,023 yards from scrimmage and eight total touchdowns on 114 carries and 85 targets in the 2025 regular season with the Steelers.

articles.headline: Part-time role expected Sunday
articles.story: Irving, who was listed as a full participant in Wednesday's walkthrough, will be slated for a part-time role if he's active for Sunday's Week 13 matchup against the Cardinals,  Scott Smith of the team's official site  reports.
articles.analysis: Offensive coordinator Josh Grizzard compared Irving's potential workload to that which Chris Godwin logged in Sunday night's loss to the Rams, when he played on 25 of 70 snaps in his return from a five-game absence due to a fibula injury. Irving has been out even longer than Godwin -- seven games -- so the abbreviated volume would certainly be within the realm of possibility, especially with two capable backfield mates in Rachaad White and Sean Tucker on hand.

articles.headline: To receive more rest in 2026
articles.story: San Francisco head coach Kyle Shanahan said Monday that "in order to have [McCaffrey] be as good as he can be throughout the whole year, we've got to get someone to help him,"  Matt Barrows of The Athletic  reports.
articles.analysis: McCaffrey earned NFL Comeback Player of the Year honors in 2025, in which he suited up for all 17 regular-season games while racking up 2,126 yards from scrimmage and 17 total touchdowns on a career-high 413 touches. Shanahan acknowledged that McCaffrey, who will turn 30 in June and was limited to just four appearances in 2024 due to Achilles and knee issues, may have had his efficiency hampered by his workhorse role in 2025, as he averaged just 3.9 YPC.  Jordan James  and  Isaac Guerendo  are current candidates to compete for an increased No. 2 role behind McCaffrey following the departure of  Brian Robinson  for Atlanta, but the 49ers also could make further additions to the backfield in April's draft.

articles.story: McCaffrey rushed 15 times for 48 yards and caught six of eight targets for 66 yards and two touchdowns in Sunday's 23-19 playoff win over the Eagles.

articles.headline: Workload could be reduced in 2026
articles.story: Coach Kyle Shanahan said Monday that the team plans to give more touches to other running backs in 2026 to keep McCaffrey fresh, per  Matt Barrows of The Athletic . "In order to have Christian be as good as he can be throughout the whole year, we've got to get someone to help him," Shanahan said.
articles.analysis: McCaffrey had a career-high 413 touches during the 2025 regular season. He led the NFL in touches for the third time in his career, having also done so with 403 touches in 2019 and 339 touches in 2023. The heavy workload seemed to catch up to the star running back's effectiveness on the ground in the playoffs, as McCaffrey was limited to 48 rushing yards on 15 carries in the wild-card round against the Eagles and 35 rushing yards on 11 carries in the NFC divisional-round loss to the Seahawks. The 49ers have Jordan James, Isaac Guerendo and Patrick Taylor as alternatives at running back on the roster, and San Francisco could bolster the position via the draft or free agency as well.

articles.headline: Being signed by Lions
articles.story: Pacheco has agreed to terms on a contract with Detroit,  Tom Pelissero of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: Pacheco fits into the Lions' backfield alongside superstar running back  Jahmyr Gibbs , replacing  David Montgomery . In his first two seasons with Kansas City, Pacheco averaged 4.7 YPC and scored 12 rushing TDs in 31 regular-season game as a bruising between-the-tackles runner. In part due to injuries, however, the 2022 seventh-round pick has now logged back-to-back seasons with less than 500 rushing yards and under 4.0 YPC, with only two rushing scores in 20 regular-season contests. Pacheco could rebound in Detroit with opposing defenses honing in on Gibbs, but he may not see the same touch volume that Montgomery did.

articles.analysis: The Kansas City running back has been out since Week 8 with a knee issue. If Pacheco is able to return for a Thanksgiving trip to Dallas, however, he'd likely still be working behind Kareem Hunt. Hunt has gotten into the end zone in four straight games while amassing 247 scrimmage yards during Pacheco's three-game absence.

articles.headline: Inks three-year deal with Dallas
articles.story: Williams (shoulder) and the Cowboys agreed to terms on a three-year, $24 million contract Saturday,  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: The new deal includes $16 million in guaranteed money and ensures that Williams doesn't enter the new league year as an unrestricted free agent. The North Carolina product inked a one-year deal with the Cowboys in March of 2025 after spending the first four years of his NFL career with the Broncos, and he enjoyed a breakout season in his first year with Dallas, when he turned 252 carries into 1,201 yards and 11 touchdowns while catching 35 passes (on 51 targets) for 137 yards and two scores over 16 regular-season games. The deal solidifies Williams as the Cowboys' RB1 while  Malik Davis  (eye) and 2025 fifth-rounder  Jaydon Blue  both operate in rotational roles.

articles.headline: Pushing Dallas into tough decision
articles.story: The Cowboys will have a tough decision to make regarding Williams' future with the team this offseason,  Todd Archer of ESPN  reports.
articles.analysis: Williams is having his best year as a pro, both in terms of his total yards from scrimmage and on a per-touch basis. That would suggest that the Cowboys would want to back him back, but the team isn't expected to exceed its budget to retain him. Recent success with RBs on cheap contracts may also contribute to a decision to let Williams walk, as Dallas got solid production from Rico Dowdle in 2024.

articles.headline: Muted production in defeat
articles.story: Hunt rushed five times for 14 yards and caught three of four targets for 21 yards in Sunday's 14-12 loss to the Raiders.
articles.analysis: Hunt was able to play in Week 18 after opening the week on the sidelines at practice with an illness, but he finished with fewer than 15 rushing yards for the third time in four weeks. The running back was able to match his season-high three catches, though, finishing third on the team in receiving yards. Hunt completed his campaign having turned 163 carries into 611 yards and eight touchdowns while reeling in 18 of 25 targets for 143 yards and a touchdown over 17 regular-season contests. The 30-year-old will enter the offseason as a free agent, and he's proven to be a rather reliable short-yardage option in the backfield, potentially making him a desirable player on the open market.

articles.analysis: Pacheco has missed Kansas City's past three games due to a knee injury. During that stretch, Hunt took on the leading role in Kansas City's backfield, scoring a touchdown in each game while averaging 19.7 touches per game. Prior to Pacheco's injury, Hunt and Pacheco formed a backfield platoon in which Hunt averaged 8.8 touches per game.

articles.analysis: Pollard was unable to keep his recent level of play going, falling below 95 yards from scrimmage for the first time since Week 13. He still dominated work out of the Titans' backfield, however, and found enough running room to set a new career high of 1,082 rushing yards for the season. Pollard is set to enter the final year of his current contract with the Titans and is likely to remain the team's lead back in 2026.

articles.headline: Bonus incentive in play Week 18
articles.story: Pollard needs 66 more rushing yards against the Jaguars on Sunday to reach 1,100 yards for the season and earn a $250,000 bonus,  Zak Koeppel of NFL.com  reports.
articles.analysis: Pollard cracked 85-plus rushing yards only once over the Titans' first 12 games of the season, but the veteran running back has reached that mark in each of his last four games, including 100-plus yards in three of those contests. He's cracked the 1,000-yard rushing mark for a fourth straight season, and in addition to the incentive on the line for Week 18, he needs 46 more yards to surpass his career high that he set in 2024 across 16 regular-season games. That won't come easy Sunday, as the Jaguars' defense allows just 86.1 rushing yards per game to opposing offenses this season, which is best in the NFL.

articles.analysis: Slayton was able to reel in at least four passes for the first time since Week 15 and the fifth time overall this season. The wide receiver finished his seventh campaign in New York having caught 37 of 64 targets for 538 yards and one touchdown over 14 regular-season contests. Slayton will enter the offseason as a free agent, and his 14.5 yards per reception this year will likely attract some potential new suitors ahead of the 2026 campaign.

articles.analysis: The veteran receiver suffered the injury late in the Week 5 loss to the Saints, and after being listed as a nonparticipant throughout the short allotment of Week 6 prep, Slayton is now officially ruled out. Raanan reports Slayton still had a very noticeable limp in the locker room Tuesday, and it remains to be seen if he'll even be healthy enough to suit up for a Week 7 road matchup against the Broncos on Sunday, Oct. 19.

articles.headline: Quiet finish to rookie season
articles.story: Egbuka brought in one of three targets for eight yards in the Buccaneers' 16-14 win over the Panthers on Saturday. He finished the 2025 regular season with 63 receptions for 938 yards and six touchdowns on 127 targets and two rushes for nine yards over 17 games.
articles.analysis: Egbuka was thrust into a significant role from Day 1 of his rookie season, as the Buccaneers began the campaign without  Chris Godwin  and  Jalen McMillan  due to ankle and neck injuries, respectively. The Ohio State product looked like an instant star after posting a 25-445-5 line on 38 targets over his first five career games, but Egbuka topped 64 receiving yards just once in the remaining 12 contests despite  Mike Evans  and Godwin missing significant time,  and McMillan not making his season debut until Week 15. Nevertheless, there isn't any doubt about Egbuka's talent after he still nearly eclipsed the 1,000-yard mark in his first season, and he could be set for a similar elevated target share in 2026 if Godwin isn't retained this offseason.

articles.story: Egbuka is a top-two wide receiver on the 2025 NFL All-Rookie team compiled by  Dane Brugler of The Athletic .
articles.analysis: Egbuka held onto a starting spot on Brugler's team despite a poor second half of the season, as Egbuka didn't score a touchdown during the final eight games of the 2025 campaign and reached 50 receiving yards in only one game during that stretch. The first half of Egbuka's rookie season was electric, though, and the 19th overall pick in the 2025 NFL Draft still finished second among all first-year players with 938 receiving yards and six touchdown catches.

articles.headline: Just two catches in Week 18 win
articles.story: Jeudy caught two of six targets for 17 yards and added five yards on his only carry in Sunday's 20-18 win over the Bengals.
articles.analysis: While the veteran wideout's volume was poor, Jeudy's final catch of the game was huge -- he converted a third down with an 11-yard grab from  Shedeur Sanders  to help set up the game-winning field goal in the final minute of the fourth quarter. Jeudy  had arguably the worst season of his NFL career in 2025, playing all 17 games but managing a poor 50-602-2 line on 106 targets as the Browns cycled through three different starting QBs. His prospects of a rebound in 2026 will depend on whether the organization can find a real solution under center this offseason.

He spent the previous four seasons with the Broncos but failed to reach 1,000 receiving yards in any of those regular seasons. Jeudy projects to operate as the top option in Cleveland's passing game in 2025, just as he did in 2024 after the team traded away Amari Cooper.

articles.headline: Suffers concussion late in loss
articles.story: Loveland suffered a concussion in Sunday night's 20-17 overtime loss to the Rams in the NFC divisional round,  Nicholas Moreano of Marquee Sports Network  reports.
articles.analysis: Loveland reeled in an 18-yard pass in the late moments of the fourth quarter, but he took a blow to the head in the process, and he exited the game. After the loss, head coach Ben Johnson confirmed that the tight end suffered a concussion on the hit. Loveland still led the Bears with 56 receiving yards, reeling in four of a team-high 10 targets in the defeat. Following an impressive regular season, the rookie tight end caught 12 of 25 targets for 193 yards over Chicago's two postseason contests.

Loveland set a new career high with three catches in Sunday's win over New Orleans.

articles.headline: Primed for larger role in 2026?
articles.story: Andrews said Wednesday that he expects "a lot of opportunities in this offense" after fellow tight ends  Isaiah Likely  and  Charlie Kolar  left the Ravens this offseason,  NFL.com  reports.
articles.analysis: More help is probably on the way, but so far Baltimore has only signed blocking specialist  Durham Smythe  to help replace Likely (Giants) and Kolar (Chargers) at tight end. The Ravens also lost long-time fullback  Patrick Ricard , who followed Likely and coach John Harbaugh to New York. Andrews, meanwhile, is entering the first season of the three-year extension he signed in December, with his 2026 compensation fully guaranteed and his 2027 salary partially guaranteed ($5 million out of $9 million). The Ravens clearly have him ticketed for a key role under new offensive coordinator Declan Doyle, but that doesn't mean Andrews will approach his peak volume (or production). He'll turn 31 in September, coming off back-to-back seasons with less than 4.5 targets and 40 yards per game. Even the

articles.headline: Gets lucrative extension Wednesday
articles.story: Andrews agreed to a three-year extension with the Ravens on Wednesday,  Jamison Hensley and Adam Schefter of ESPN  report.
articles.analysis: The veteran tight end was slated for free agency this coming offseason, but Andrews is now locked in with the Ravens through the 2028 season. The 30-year-old's extension has a total value of $39.3 million, including $26 million guaranteed. Andrews is now the team's career leader in receptions, receiving yards and touchdown catches after being selected in the third round of the 2018 NFL Draft.

articles.headline: Restructures contract
articles.story: Hockenson (shoulder) and the Vikings agreed to a restructured contract Saturday that lowers his cap number for the 2026 season by $5 million,  Tom Pelissero of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: Minnesota continues to get closer to cap compliance with this move, getting Hockenson's league-high $21.3 million cap hit (among tight ends) down to a more palatable $16.3 million, per  Kevin Seifert of ESPN.com . Hockenson missed the final two games of the 2025 regular season due to a shoulder injury, finishing it with 51 catches (on 66 targets) for 438 yards and three touchdowns in 15 appearances. He and  Josh Oliver  (ankle) will continue to operate as the Vikings' top two tight ends in the upcoming campaign.

articles.headline: Might be cut loose
articles.story: The Vikings could choose to cut Hockenson this offseason,  Aaron Schatz of ESPN  suggests.
articles.analysis: It's difficult to evaluate the Vikings' pass catchers from 2025 due to inconsistent quarterback play. Hockenson was among the players seemingly affected, as he averaged only 8.6 yards per catch --- the lowest mark of his career -- while also posting his lowest yardage total since his rookie campaign. He has two years remaining on his current contract with a total cap hit of $44.7 million, and cutting him would only result in a $12.5 million dead cap hit for the team.

articles.headline: Extended by Houston
articles.story: Fairbairn and the Texans have agreed on a two-year deal,  Mike Garafolo of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: The contract is a two-year, $13 million extension, which makes Fairbairn the highest-paid kicker in the NFL. The 32-year-old made NFL history during the 2025 season by tying David Akers' 2011 record for most field goals in a single season with 44. This extension will provide key continuity on special teams for the Texans as they chase a fourth consecutive playoff berth in the 2026 season.

Wright filled in capably, but Fairbairn's lack of a Week 12 injury designation coupled with Wright's departure from the roster point to Fairbairn returning Thursday. Fairbairn was one of the league's most effective kickers prior to his injury, connecting on 22 of 25 field-goal attempts in eight games played.

articles.headline: Reaches deal with Las Vegas
articles.story: Cousins agreed Thursday with the Raiders on a contract,  Josh Kendall of The Athletic  reports.
articles.analysis: After being cut by the Falcons earlier in the offseason, Cousins had drawn interest from several teams who were keen on bringing him aboard as a backup. He ultimately opted to head to Las Vegas, where he'll at least temporarily slot in atop the depth chart. The Raiders are widely expected to select Indiana quarterback  Fernando Mendoza  with the No. 1 overall pick in the draft later this month, but Cousins -- who is in line to earn $20 million fully guaranteed for playing the 2026 season -- per  Tom Pelissero of NFL Network,  could still emerge as the Week 1 starter if Las Vegas isn't eager to rush Mendoza into action right away to begin his NFL career. articles.headline: Right attitude in new Vegas digs
articles.story: Cousins, who signed with the Raiders on April 2, said Wednesday he's fully embracing the notion of the team playing the best quarterback on the roster, irrespective of whether it's him or not,  Ryan McFadden of ESPN.com  reports.
articles.analysis: Cousins' comments may be relatively unsurprising on the surface, considering the Raiders are expected to select Heisman Trophy winner Fernando Mendoza from Indiana with the top overall pick in the 2026 NFL Draft. However, they do carry some extra significance coming from the veteran signal-caller, considering he'd signed a multi-year deal with the Falcons in 2024 only to see Atlanta surprisingly invest a first-round pick in Michael Penix just a month later. Cousins naturally has clear eyes with respect to his new situation, saying, "If that's not me, I don't want to be out there. I don't think that's the best thing for the team."

articles.analysis: The 33-year-old QB is signing a one-year contract with Washington for the third consecutive offseason. He was one of the league's better backup quarterbacks the past couple of years, and there should be a degree of continuity with internal promotion David Blough replacing Kliff Kingsbury as the offensive coordinator.

articles.headline: Set to start for rest of year
articles.story: Mariota should remain the starting quarterback for the Commanders for the rest of the 2025 season after  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network  reports that the team has elected to shut down Jayden Daniels.
articles.analysis: Daniels returned from a dislocated non-throwing elbow in Week 14 against the Vikings but lasted only 39 snaps before being removed following a hit that had him showing visible pain. As it turns out, that will be his last appearance of the year, as Washington is already eliminated from playoff contention and has no reason to risk further injury to its young franchise QB. Mariota has been a solid replacement, completing 62.0 percent of his passes with a 10:7 TD:INT and 7.5 YPA in 10 games while adding 298 yards and a touchdown on the ground. Mariota will be backed up by 39-year-old Josh Johnson over the final three weeks.

articles.headline: Plays workhorse role in loss
articles.story: Estime rushed 21 times for 63 yards and brought in all five targets for 43 yards in the Saints' 19-17 loss to the Falcons on Sunday.
articles.analysis: With  Alvin Kamara  (knee) sidelined once again to close out the season, Estime easily paced the Saints' ground attack in carries and rushing yards for a second straight week. The second-year pro was much less efficient than during his 14-carry, 94-yard performance against the Titans in Week 17, but Estime helped make up for it with an impressive day as a pass catcher. Estime put some good play on tape over the last two games, and with Kamara's future uncertain, it's possible Estime enters the final year of his deal with New Orleans in position to compete for significant playing time in 2026.

articles.story: Estime will probably continue to lead the Saints' backfield in Week 18 against the Falcons, as Alvin Kamara  remained sidelined  at Thursday's practice due to knee and ankle injuries.
articles.analysis: Kamara has missed five consecutive games and is trending toward another absence as he has yet to return to practice. After Devin Neal suffered a season-ending hamstring injury, Estime ceded the majority of rushing opportunities to Taysom Hill in Week 16 against the Jets before taking over as the lead back in Week 17 against the Titans. After rushing 14 times for 94 yards and a touchdown while bringing in his only target for four yards against Tennessee, Estime is poised to retain the lead back role for the regular-season finale against the Falcons.

articles.headline: Held in check in season finale
articles.story: Taylor carried the ball 14 times for 26 yards and caught both his targets for 13 yards in Sunday's 38-30 loss to the Texans.
articles.analysis: It was the least productive game of the season for Taylor, as his longest run went for only eight yards and rookie  DJ Giddens  wound up leading the Colts with 30 rushing yards. Taylor missed his chance to catch  James Cook  for the NFL rushing crown, but he still wraps up an impressive campaign with 1,585 rushing yards and a career-high 18 TDs on the ground, along with a 46-378-2 line on 55 targets through the air. The 26-year-old RB will head into 2026 as the centerpiece of the Indianapolis offense.

articles.headline: Catches late TD in Super Bowl 60
articles.story: Stevenson rushed the ball seven times for 23 yards in Sunday's 29-13 loss to the Seahawks in Super Bowl LX. He added five receptions (on five targets) for 40 yards and a touchdown.
articles.analysis: Stevenson began the game as a key part of New England's game plan, though the team's inability to maintain drives limited his impact. All seven of his carries came in the first half, and the majority of his production as a pass catcher came with the Patriots down by multiple scores. The highlights of Stevenson's performance were a long reception of 13 yards and a seven-yard touchdown grab just before the two-minute warning. Stevenson fended off  TreVeyon Henderson  as the lead back in 2025, though Stevenson may lose a bit more of his volume, as the duo projects to split the Patriots backfield again in 2026.

The Patriots were also likely encouraged by the fact there's a more-than-capable replacement in rookie TreVeyon Henderson, who recorded a career-high 147 rushing yards against the Buccaneers on Sunday.

articles.headline: Sees 58 targets in eight Jets games
articles.story: Mitchell caught 33 of 74 targets for 453 yards and two touchdowns while rushing once for minus-4 yards in 16 games split between the Colts and Jets.
articles.analysis: Mitchell played eight games with each team in 2025 but saw a major uptick in usage after being traded to the Jets, catching 24 of 58 targets for 301 yards and his first two NFL touchdowns with New York. His abhorrent 43.4 percent catch rate (56 catches on 129 targets) through two NFL seasons is a red flag, but subpar quarterback play has certainly contributed to that ugly statistic, though Mitchell has played his part with drops and mental mistakes. The 2024 second-round pick's ability to get open is intriguing, and there's a path to fantasy relevance in 2026 if the Jets improve their QB situation, as Mitchell's favored to hold onto a starting role on the outside, barring the addition of a starting caliber wideout in free agency or the draft.

articles.analysis: Mitchell is currently penciled into the second spot on the Jets' wide receiver depth chart behind Garrett Wilson, though it wouldn't be surprising to see the Jets add a wide receiver capable of competing for a starting role in the draft. Smith should offer an upgrade under center compared to what the Jets got in 2025 from a combination of Brady Cook, Tyrod Taylor and Justin Fields, the last of whom was sidelined by a knee injury down the stretch. Mitchell had 301 receiving yards in eight games with the Jets after being acquired from the Colts in a midseason trade.

articles.headline: Productive following IR return
articles.story: Watson finished the 2025 regular season with 35 catches (on 55 targets) for 611 yards and six touchdowns across 10 games.
articles.analysis: Watson played a key role on the Packers' offense during Saturday's 31-27 NFC wild-card loss to the Bears, finishing with three catches (on seven targets) for 39 yards which included a seven-yard touchdown pass from  Jordan Love  late in the first quarter. Watson missed the first seven games of the regular season while recovering from a torn ACL that he suffered in Week 18 of the 2024 campaign. Despite the missed time, the fourth-year pro finished the regular season second on the Packers in catches, targets and yards behind  Romeo Doubs . Even with Green Bay bringing in rookie  Matthew Golden , Watson still played a key part in the offense, particularly after  Tucker Kraft  tore his ACL in Week 9 against the Panthers. articles.headline: Recovering quickly
articles.story: Watson is likely to begin the season on the PUP list but could return shortly after Week 4,  Ryan Wood of USA Today  reports.
articles.analysis: Watson tore his ACL in Week 18 of the 2024 season, so it's little surprise he'll miss the start of the 2025 campaign. However, he has reportedly impressed the coaching staff with the speed of his recovery and was seen doing resistance work on the side fields Tuesday. It's unclear what type of role Watson will return to, as rookie Matthew Golden has drawn strong reviews during training camp while both Romeo Doubs and Jayden Reed already have established roles in the offense.

articles.headline: Logs 4-89-1 line in NFCCG loss
articles.story: Adams finished with four receptions on six targets for 89 yards and a touchdown in Sunday's 31-27 loss to the Seahawks in the NFC Championship Game.
articles.analysis: Adams finally looked healthy in the conference championship round after suffering an injury to his hamstring in Week 15 of the regular season. The 32-year-old superstar finished with a catch rate north of 50 percent for the first time in three starts this postseason while also registering his first playoff score as a member of the Rams. Adams had a renaissance year with Los Angeles in 2025, finishing the regular season with 14 touchdown receptions in as many appearances, tying the second-highest mark of his storied 13-year career. The Rams' passing offense should have a similar makeup in 2026 with QB  Matthew Stafford  under center and  Puka Nacua  and Adams starring on the outside.

articles.headline: Likely returning to Rams
articles.story: Adams is expected to be back with the Rams in 2026,  Sarah Barshop of ESPN.com  reports.
articles.analysis: The veteran wide receiver signed a two-year contract with Los Angeles before last season, but it was structured in such a way that the team would have an easy out this offseason if Adams hadn't proven to be a good fit. Instead, he posted a 60-789-14 line over 14 regular-season games while shining as a red-zone target for Matthew Stafford, adding one more score in the NFC Championship Game loss to the Seahawks. It's possible the Rams will look to re-negotiate Adams' deal and reduce his cap hit, however, as the team attempts to create space for a Puka Nacua extension.

articles.headline: Team-high six catches in loss
articles.story: Higgins caught six of 10 targets for 59 yards in the Texans' 28-16 loss to the Patriots in the divisional round Sunday.
articles.analysis: The rookie wideout led Houston in catches and receiving yards with  Nico Collins  (concussion) unavailable, but it was  Christian Kirk  who caught  C.J. Stroud 's only TD pass of the day. Higgins put together a respectable rookie season after being a second-round pick out of Iowa State, playing all 17 regular-season games and posting a 41-525-6 line on 68 targets. With Kirk a free agent this offseason, Higgins may have shown enough to dissuade the Texans from bringing in veteran competition for the No. 2 receiver role.

articles.headline: Top outside WR in divisional round?
articles.story: Higgins is likely to work as the top outside wide receiver for the Texans in the AFC divisional round against the Patriots, as  DJ Bien-Aime of ESPN.com  reports that Nico Collins wasn't spotted at practice Thursday and remains in concussion protocol.
articles.analysis: Collins is trending toward sitting out Sunday's road playoff game against the Patriots after sustaining a concussion in the wild-card round against the Steelers. Higgins finished second among Houston's wide receivers with 525 receiving yards in the regular season while tying Collins for the team lead with six touchdown catches, though slot receiver Christian Kirk was the team's most effective pass-catcher against the Steelers. The Patriots' top cornerback, Christian Gonzalez, is also in concussion protocol, though he's ahead of Collins in his recovery.

articles.headline: Simple contract restructure
articles.story: Jefferson agreed to terms Friday on a contract restructure that frees up salary cap space for the Vikings,  Tom Pelissero of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: Jefferson and left tackle  Christian Darrisaw  (knee) both agreed to restructures Friday, helping Minnesota achieve a more manageable salary cap situation ahead of the start of the legal tampering period on Monday. The star wide receiver, who will turn 27 in June, will likely spend the offseason focusing on building an increased rapport with quarterback  J.J. McCarthy  (hand), after the two struggled to get on the same page throughout the 2025 campaign. The Vikings have also been linked to numerous quarterbacks expected to be available in free agency as potential added competition for McCarthy.

articles.headline: Possible target for Bills
articles.story: Jordan Dajani of CBSSports.com  predicts that the Bills could try to trade for Jefferson in the offseason.
articles.analysis: Buffalo's new head coach Joe Brady coached Jefferson, along with Joe Burrow and Ja'Marr Chase, as the offensive coordinator on LSU's 2019 championship team. The Bills' continued playoff failures could lead the team to try to shake things up by acquiring a star target for Josh Allen, and Jefferson struggled to build chemistry with Vikings quarterback J.J. McCarthy in 2025 while finishing with just 1,048 receiving yards, which is Jefferson's lowest total in six NFL regular seasons. Even with Jefferson coming off a down season, it would take a substantial package for Minnesota to consider moving him, potentially involving multiple first-round draft picks.

articles.headline: Headed to Steelers
articles.story: The Colts are trading Pittman to the Steelers,  Adam Schefter of ESPN  reports.
articles.analysis: In the deal, the Colts will receive a sixth-round draft pick in 2026 in exchange for the wideout and a 2026 seventh-rounder. Pittman, who is coming off a 2025 regular-season in which he caught 80 of his 111 targets for 784 yards and a career-high seven touchdowns in 17 contests, will provide the Steelers' WR corps with an immediate boost, as he'll work alongside  DK Metcalf  this coming season. Per  Adam Schefter of ESPN , dealing Pittman will free up $24 million in salary-cap space for the Colts, who signed fellow WR  Alec Pierce  to a reported four-year, $116 million deal earlier Monday. Additionally,  Mike Garafolo of NFL Network  reports that Pittman is getting a new three-year, $59 million deal with the Steelers, but at this stage it's uncertain which QB he'll be catching passes from

articles.headline: Likely cut if no restructure
articles.story: Pittman is likely to restructure his contract with the Colts or be released in the offseason, per  The Athletic NFL Staff .
articles.analysis: Pittman is set to carry a $29 million cap hit in 2026, which is unacceptably high for a player who recorded just 784 receiving yards in 2025. The Colts could be interested in keeping Pittman around, especially if Alec Pierce signs elsewhere in free agency, but Pittman would probably have to bring down his cap hit with a restructured deal to avoid being released, which would offer $24 million in cap relief.

articles.headline: Strong production in 2025
articles.story: Henry caught 60 of 87 targets for 768 yards and seven touchdowns over 17 games during the 2025 regular season.
articles.analysis: Henry was able to appear in all 17 of the team's regular-season contests for the first time since 2022, and he notched a career-high 768 receiving yards. His seven touchdowns were his highest total since 2021, and he reeled in at least 60 passes for the second straight year and third time overall in his 10-year NFL career. The tight end also caught nine of 15 targets for 112 yards and a score over the Patriots' four games during the postseason. Henry has developed into being quite a reliable option for quarterback  Drake Maye , and he'll look to keep it up in 2026.

articles.headline: Needs five catches for bonus
articles.story: Henry needs five receptions against the Dolphins on Sunday to reach 60 for the season and receive a $250,000 bonus,  Zak Koeppel of NFL.com  reports.
articles.analysis: Henry has caught at least five catches in just three of 16 regular-season games, but he's reached that threshold in two of his last five outings. The Patriots are facing a Dolphins defense that has allowed the third-most receptions to tight ends this year, and there shouldn't be too much of a concern over New England resting its starters as it battles for the No. 1 seed in the AFC. Even if he doesn't reach his incentive, Henry has put together a strong 2025 campaign, with his 712 receiving yards marking a career high for the 2016 second-rounder out of Arkansas.

articles.headline: Finishes second in NFL with 126 catches
articles.story: McBride had seven receptions on eight targets for 65 yards in Sunday's 37-20 loss to the Rams.
articles.analysis: Fresh off setting a new league record for catches by a tight end in a single season last week, McBride shifted his focus on Sunday to the league lead in receptions for all position players. Arizona's star tight end entered Week 18 in a tie for first place with Rams wideout  Puka Nacua , but it was the latter who finished the contest with more receptions and first-place honors. McBride's 126 grabs across 17 starts were still good enough to earn second place, finishing one catch ahead of Cincinnati's  Ja'Marr Chase  this regular season. The 25-year-old McBride corrected the red-zone woes that plagued him early in his career (six TDs in first three seasons) with a whopping 11 trips to the end zone in 2025. The breakout fantasy star accomplished all of this while catching passes from two diffe

articles.headline: NFC's starting TE in Pro Bowl
articles.story: McBride is the starting tight end for the NFC in the Pro Bowl,  Jenna West of The Athletic  reports.
articles.analysis: McBride has yet another accolade to add to a superb 2025 season in which he set the single-season catch record for his position with 119. George Kittle was the other tight end voted to the Pro Bowl for the NFC, but Kittle suffered an Achilles injury and will be replaced by Jake Ferguson behind McBride in Tuesday's Pro Bowl flag football game. This is the second consecutive Pro Bowl season for McBride.

articles.headline: Makes lone kick in Week 18 loss
articles.story: Dicker made his lone field goal during the Chargers' 19-3 loss to the Broncos on Sunday.
articles.analysis: The Chargers struggled to generate any offense Sunday with  Trey Lance  under center instead of  Justin Herbert  (coach's decision), but Dicker put the team on the board with a 30-yard chip shot as time expired in the first half. It was another productive season for Dicker, as he went 38-for-41 (92.7 percent) on field-goal attempts over 16 regular-season games to finish third in the NFL in made field goals behind  Ka'imi Fairbairn  (44) and  Jason Myers  (41). However, Dicker connected on just five of six field-goal attempts from 50-plus yards, which was his lowest over the last three years.

articles.headline: Nabs lucrative extension
articles.story: Dicker signed a four-year, $22 million contract extension with the Chargers on Friday,  Kris Rhim of ESPN.com  reports.
articles.analysis: The deal includes $12.5 million guaranteed according to Rhim's ESPN colleague Adam Schefter. It's a well-earned payday for the fourth-year kicker, who knocked home 31 of 33 field-goal attempts, including seven of nine from 50-plus yards, and all 35 extra-point tries in 2023 across 17 regular-season games. Dicker initially played 10 regular-season games with the Chargers in 2022 and then beat out veteran Dustin Hopkins for the placekicking job during the 2023 preseason.

articles.headline: Ahead of schedule, per team owner
articles.story: Broncos owner Greg Penner said Monday that Nix is "ahead of schedule" in his recovery from right ankle surgery,  Chris Tomasson of The Denver Gazette  reports.
articles.analysis: Penner said in January that he had "zero concern" about Nix's recovery from a bone break in his right ankle, and the team owner echoed those comments Monday. "He's attacked his recovery in the same way that he attacks preparing for games and has just done a terrific job," Penner said of Nix. "He's ahead of schedule. No concerns at all for OTAs and go forward from there." Denver coach Sean Payton stated in February that he expects Broncos OTAs to start June 1, and Nix is trending toward being ready to participate by that date.

articles.headline: To be back on field in 4-to-6 weeks
articles.story: Nix said Wednesday that he'll begin training again in about 4-to-6 weeks after undergoing surgery on a "simple bone break" in his right ankle last Tuesday,  Parker Gabriel of The Denver Post  reports.

articles.headline: Clutch performance in playoff win

articles.headline: Expected to be ready for OTAs
articles.story: Broncos owner Greg Penner said Monday that Nix is expected to be available by the start of OTAs in June, per  Chris Tomasson of The Denver Gazette . "He's ahead of schedule," Penner said of Nix's recovery from right ankle surgery. "No concerns at all for OTAs."
articles.analysis: Nix broke a bone in his right ankle at the end of Denver's win over the Bills in the AFC divisional round in January. That injury likely cost the Broncos a trip to the Super Bowl, as Denver lost 10-7 to the Patriots in the AFC Championship Game, with New England's lone touchdown coming directly following a controversial fumble called on backup quarterback Jarrett Stidham. With Nix expected to be available with plenty of time to spare prior to the 206 regular season, the Broncos are projected to be one of the top teams in the AFC again.

articles.headline: Fifth-year option getting picked up
articles.story: General manager Dan Morgan announced Tuesday that Carolina plans to exercise the 2027 team option on Young's contract this offseason,  Darin Gantt of the Panthers' official site  reports.
articles.analysis: Young's fifth-year option is projected to come in at around $26.5 million and will be fully guaranteed. While the Panthers' willingness to pick up the option represents some level of commitment to the 24-year-old signal-caller, Young may need to make more dramatic strides in 2026 in order to land a long-term extension. He showed some modest improvement in Year 3, leading an 8-9 Panthers squad to its first NFC South title in a decade while directing six game-winning drives. Young completed 304 of 478 pass attempts for 3,011 yards, 23 touchdowns and 11 interceptions to go with 216 yards and two scores on 54 carries over 16 starts during the regular season. A respectable showing against the heavily favored Rams in the wild-card round this past weekend will also be a confidence booster for the

articles.story: Young completed 21 of 40 passes for 264 yards, one touchdown and one interception in Saturday's 34-31 wild-card loss to the Rams. He added three rushes for 24 yards and an additional score.

articles.headline: Not likely to get long-term offer
articles.story: The Panthers are not likely to offer Young a long-term extension prior to the 2026 season,  Joseph Person of The Athletic  reports.
articles.analysis: Carolina picked up Young's fifth-year option on his rookie contract, meaning he'll be with the team through 2027. However, the team is not yet ready to commit to him beyond that point, as the Panthers want every chance to evaluate Young. While he led the team to the playoffs in 2025 and set a new career high with 23 touchdowns across 16 regular-season games, Young has never averaged more than 6.3 yards per attempt in a campaign and often struggles to connect with receivers over the middle of the field due to his short stature.

articles.headline: Finds end zone in season finale
articles.story: Brown carried the ball 13 times for 72 yards and caught four of six targets for 18 yards and a touchdown in Sunday's 20-18 loss to the Browns.
articles.analysis: The rushing yardage gave Brown his first 1,000-yard campaign on the ground, while the first-quarter TD gave him a total of 11 on the season (six rushing, five receiving) to tie his career high. The third-year RB also set a new personal best with 1,456 scrimmage yards, and he'll head into the final year of his rookie contract in 2026 locked into the top spot in the Cincy backfield.

articles.analysis: Jones-Drew doesn't expect significant production from Brown, projecting Cincinnati to rely primarily on its passing game. Brown had a quiet rookie season in 2023 but broke out in 2024, finding plenty of room to contribute on the ground with 990 rushing yards and seven rushing touchdowns, in addition to catching four of Joe Burrow's league-high 43 touchdown passes.

articles.headline: Joining Seattle's backfield
articles.story: The Seahawks are slated to sign Wilson to a one-year deal worth up to $2.1 million,  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: While working as  Josh Jacobs ' top backup with Green Bay in 2025, Wilson recorded 496 yards and three TDs on 125 carries, along with 99 yards on 15 catches in 17 regular-season games. With  Kenneth Walker  departing via free agency and  Zach Charbonnet  having suffered a torn ACL in January, Wilson could potentially earn a key early-season role in a Seattle backfield that also includes  George Holani  and  Kenny McIntosh , though it's possible that the team adds further RB depth in the coming weeks and months.

articles.headline: Could get more Week 15 carries
articles.story: Wilson could see an expanded workload Sunday against the Broncos, as  Matt Schneidman of The Athletic  reports that Josh Jacobs didn't practice Thursday and is day-to-day due to a knee injury.
articles.analysis: Jacobs missed Week 12 due to a knee injury and Wilson excelled while making a spot start, rushing for 107 yards and two touchdowns against the Vikings. The same knee apparently stiffened up on Jacobs in Week 14 against the Bears, putting his status for Week 15 against the Broncos in doubt. Denver boasts an elite run defense, but Wilson would likely handle a heavy workload nonetheless if Jacobs doesn't play Sunday.

articles.headline: Can't get going in Week 18
articles.story: Croskey-Merritt recorded 13 rushes for 29 yards in Sunday's 24-17 win over the Eagles.
articles.analysis: Croskey-Merritt saw eight of his 13 carries on the Commanders' opening possession, though he was phased out of the offense from there after picking up only five total yards. This performance was indicative of an up-and-down rookie season for Croskey-Merritt, as he reached at least 95 rushing yards in two of his final six games but failed to reach 35 yards on the ground in the other four. The Commanders are a candidate to add a running back either in free agency or the draft this offseason, but Croskey-Merritt is likely to have a role in 2026.

articles.headline: Team adds Ford and White at RB
articles.story: Croskey-Merritt is set to be part of a new-look Commanders backfield, as  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network  reports that Washington agreed to a one-year contract with Jerome Ford on Saturday, and  John Keim of ESPN.com  reports that the team agreed to a one-year deal with Rachaad White on Thursday.
articles.analysis: Croskey-Merritt led the Commanders with 805 yards on 175 carries as a rookie in 2025 while Chris Rodriguez and Jeremy McNichols combined for 721 rushing yards on 156 carries. Rodriguez has signed with the Jaguars while McNichols remains a free agent. White and Ford have both played primarily in platoon roles, though White had 272 carries for 990 yards in the lead role for the Buccaneers in the 2023 regular season. If the Commanders don't bolster their backfield further in the draft, Croskey-Merritt would be in good position to maintain a prominent role as a rusher, though he showed minimal pass-catching ability with just nine receptions in 2025.

articles.headline: Stymied by Bears in playoff loss
articles.story: Jacobs rushed 19 times for 55 yards and brought in one of three targets for one yard in the Packers' 31-27 wild-card loss to the Bears on Saturday. He also returned two kickoffs for 61 yards and committed a fumble recovered by Green Bay.
articles.analysis: After getting Week 18 off for rest, Jacobs came into Saturday's postseason matchup refreshed, but a Bears defense that had been vulnerable to the run at times was highly effective in cutting off his rushing lanes. Jacobs' long run of the night went for a modest 13 yards, meaning he netted just 42 yards on his other 18 carries. Jacobs had a quiet finish to the regular season as well since he generated just a 16-39-0 rushing line in his last two contests, and he finished the 2025 campaign having amassed 929 rushing yards (4.0 yards per carry), a 36-282-1 tally through the air on 44 targets and three fumbles (two lost) across 15 regular-season games.

articles.headline: Refreshed heading into postseason
articles.story: Jacobs, who did not play in Week 18 for rest purposes, believes the down time will pay off in Saturday's wild-card matchup against the Bears,  Rob Demovsky of ESPN.com  reports.
articles.analysis: Jacobs has been dealing with a lingering knee issue since mid-November, so the week off, coupled with what tied as his lightest snap count and lowest touch count of the season in Week 17, appear to have made a significant impact. "My body coming into this game is the best I've felt probably in the last six weeks," Jacobs said. "Pretty good situation to be in."

articles.headline: Staying with Arizona
articles.story: Knight is re-signing with the Cardinals on a one-year contract,  Mike Garafolo of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: Knight was Arizona's lead runner at one point in 2025, but only after  James Conner  (foot) and  Trey Benson  (knee) both suffered season-ending injuries. Conner and Benson still are under contract, now joined by free-agent addition  Tyler Allgeier . It doesn't look good for Knight landing any higher than third or fourth on the depth chart come training camp. He should at least be healthy, having reportedly avoided surgery on the ankle injury that ended his 2025 season in December.

articles.headline: Shut down for season
articles.story: The Cardinals placed Knight (ankle) on injured reserve Tuesday,  Zach Gershman of the team's official site  reports.

articles.headline: Likely lead back for rest of 2025
articles.story: Knight is poised to operate as the top option in Arizona's backfield on early downs for the remainder of the 2025 season, as  Darren Urban of the Cardinals' official site  reports that Trey Benson will spend the remainder of the 2025 season on injured reserve due to a knee injury.
articles.analysis: Arizona's 21-day window to activate Benson has come and gone. With Benson out of the mix, the Cardinals will continue to lean on Knight, Emari Demercado and Michael Carter at running back. Both Demercado and Carter profile as shifty change-of-pace options who can contribute in passing situations, while the 5-foot-11, 210-pound Knight has the frame to take on larger rushing workloads, having notched double-digit touches in seven of the past eight games. Demercado has missed the last three games due to an ankle injury.

articles.headline: Limited output in Week 18
articles.story: Iosivas had two catches for 10 yards on three targets in Sunday's loss to the Browns. He ended the season with 33 catches for 435 yards and two touchdowns, falling short of his 2024 production in all three categories.
articles.analysis: Despite Iosivas playing every game and teammate  Tee Higgins  missing multiple games due to injury, Iosivas fell short of expectations this season, and not all of that was due to quarterback  Joe Burrow  missing eight games with a toe injury. The Bengals used fewer sets with wide receivers, but even that doesn't explain the drop-off for Iosivas, who actually played seven more offensive snaps than last season. Nonetheless, he's expected to return for another season as the Bengals' third receiver in 2026.

articles.headline: Expanded role on tap
articles.story: Iosivas will operate as the Bengals' WR2 against the Ravens on Sunday after Tee Higgins was ruled out due to a concussion,  Ben Baby of ESPN.com  reports.
articles.analysis: Iosivas played a season-high 78 snaps on offense in Week 13 against the Ravens when Ja'Marr Chase was sidelined due to a suspension. While the former had just two targets in that game, he made the most of it by hauling in a 29-yard touchdown pass from Joe Burrow. Iosivas is in a similar spot in Sunday's AFC North rematch due to the absence of Higgins, but Chase, Mitchell Tinsley and Mike Gesicki should all see more targets from Burrow. Over his last 10 games, Iosivas has caught 25 passes on 42 targets for 351 yards and two touchdowns.

articles.headline: Joined in WR corps by Waddle
articles.story: The Broncos have officially announced their trade with the Dolphins for  Jaylen Waddle ,  Aric DiLalla of the team's official site  reports, a move that bolsters the team's wideout corps, which Sutton led last season.
articles.analysis: In 17 regular-season games in 2025, Sutton parlayed his team-leading 124 targets into a  74-1,017-7 receiving line, with  Troy Franklin  next in line in terms of targets with 104. The addition of Waddle -- a 2021 first-rounder -- gives Denver a dynamic playmaker to pair with Sutton, providing the team with a strong 1-2 punch at wideout, supplemented by Franklin,  Pat Bryant  and  Marvin Mims , with  Lil'Jordan Humphrey  along with  Michael Bandy  rounding out the current WR depth chart. As the 2026 campaign approaches, Sutton isn't necessarily the Broncos' clear-cut top option at the position, a standing that Waddle has the upside to claim should he mesh well with franchise QB  Bo Nix .

articles.story: Sutton will work with Jarrett Stidham as his quarterback in the AFC Championship Game since Bo Nix suffered a postseason-ending ankle injury in Saturday's divisional-round overtime win against the Bills,  Tom Pelissero of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: Sutton and Nix naturally enjoy plenty of chemistry, while Sutton hasn't seen a target from Stidham in a non-preseason setting since 2023. The veteran wideout just completed his third career 1,000-yard season in 2025, and he reeled in four of nine targets, all in the fourth quarter and overtime, of Saturday's win against Buffalo.

articles.headline: Set to join Giants
articles.story: Mooney is slated to sign a one-year, $10 million contract with the Giants,  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: Mooney entered free agency after he was released by the Falcons on Monday, and it didn't take him long to find a new home with the Giants. He entered the 2025 season with high expectations following a productive 2024 campaign with the Falcons, when he caught 64 passes (on 106 targets) for 992 yards and five touchdowns across 16 regular-season games. Mooney failed to catch more than four passes in each of his 15 regular-season games in 2025 and finished with a 32-443-1 receiving line (on 72 targets). He figures to slide in as the Giants' WR3 behind  Malik Nabers  (knee) and  Darius Slayton  for the upcoming season.

articles.headline: Time up in Atlanta
articles.story: Mooney will be released by the Falcons when the new league year begins March 11, per  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network .
articles.analysis: Mooney signed a three-year, $39 million contract with Atlanta prior to the 2024 season. His production declined from 992 receiving yards in 2024 to just 443 yards in 2025. The Falcons will clear $7.4 million in cap space by releasing Mooney, who is likely to garner some interest in free agency, though he'll be fighting an uphill battle to earn a starting role after his poor performance in 2025.

articles.headline: Paces pass catchers in playoff loss
articles.story: Mims brought in four of six targets for 62 yards and returned three punts for eight yards in the Broncos' 10-7 loss to the Patriots in Sunday's AFC Championship Game.
articles.analysis: Mims and the Broncos looked like they were on the way to a productive afternoon when the speedster hauled in a 52-yard pass from  Jarrett Stidham  on Denver's second possession, setting up a touchdown pass to  Courtland Sutton  two plays later. Mims did continue to feature as a key part of the air attack the remainder of the day in the absence of  Troy Franklin  and  Pat Bryant  -- the latter exiting the game in the second quarter -- due to hamstring issues, but a second-half snowstorm significantly comprised both teams' offenses. Mims finished as the co-leader in receptions and targets while setting the pace in receiving yards, capping off a productive two-game postseason run that also saw him generate an 8-93-1 line in the divisional-round overtime victory against the Bills.

articles.headline: Critical TD grab in playoff win
articles.story: Mims secured all eight targets for 93 yards and a touchdown in the Broncos' 33-30 overtime divisional-round win over the Bills on Saturday. He also returned two kickoffs for 50 yards.

articles.headline: Could be expendable
articles.story: The Broncos could look to trade Mims this offseason,  Ben Solak of ESPN  suggests.
articles.analysis: The Broncos acquired Jaylen Waddle from the Dolphins on Tuesday, leaving the team's wide receiver room crowded with Courtland Sutton, Pat Bryant, Troy Franklin and Mims also on the roster. Mims has surpassed 500 receiving yards only once in three seasons, but he is the team's primary kick and punt returner and has two career kick-return TDs in three seasons. His work on special teams could ultimately deter Denver from moving on.

articles.headline: Checks into rehab facility
articles.story: Nacua's attorney confirmed this week that the star wide receiver has checked into a rehab facility in Malibu, California,  Edward Lewis of the California Post  reports.
articles.analysis: Nacua has been in the news for the wrong reasons since the Rams' season came to an end in the NFC Championship Game against the Seahawks back in January. He was accused of biting a woman during a Dec. 31 night out in Los Angeles after consuming alcohol. The woman then pressed charges against Nacua, who has since checked into a recovery center. "He is committed to using this time constructively so that he can return in the best possible position -- both personally and professionally -- to continue contributing to his team and the game he loves," Nacua's lawyer, Levi McCathern, said via  Steve Wyche of NFL Network . "He will complete the program in time to fully participate in all of the Rams' OTAs. Puka is also deeply grateful for the support he has received from his family, friends, coach

articles.story: Nacua's attorney confirmed Wednesday that the star receiver checked into a rehab facility recently and is "scheduled to be there for a while longer," Sarah Barshop of ESPN.com  reports.
articles.analysis: Nacua's attorney, Levi McCathern, stated Nacua had been in rehab for a "substantial period of time" before recent allegations against him that led to a civil lawsuit surfaced. Nacua's attorney stated the talented wideout's purpose in entering the program was "to improve his overall behavior in every aspect of his life" and added he'd complete the program well in advance of all of the Rams' OTAs.

articles.headline: To remain in Tampa Bay
articles.story: Otton is finalizing a deal that will have him re-sign with the Buccaneers,  Ian Rapoport of NFL Network  reports.
articles.analysis: Otton, a 2022 fourth-round pick, was positioned as one of the top available tight ends on the free-agent market, but he'll instead remain in a familiar offense with quarterback  Baker Mayfield  at the helm. Otton's time in Tampa Bay has largely been characterized by lofty snap counts and low target rates, though he's proven capable of handling larger receiving workloads when injuries at WR necessitate it. He made 58 regular-season starts over the course of his rookie contract, posting his best receiving line in 2024 (59-600-4) despite missing three games. Otton dropped to 59-572-1 last season, with six fewer targets (81) in two more games (16).

articles.headline: New home in Denver?
articles.story: Otton would give the Broncos a top run-blocking tight end that would also serve as a complementary piece in the passing game along with veteran Evan Engram,  Aaron Schatz of ESPN.com  relays.
articles.analysis: Otton has provided consistent production for the Buccaneers since being selected in the fourth round of the 2022 NFL Draft. He's caught 59 passes in back-to-back regular seasons and has logged at least 450 receiving yards in each of his last three campaigns, though his one receiving touchdown across 16 games in 2025 was the lowest mark of his career. Broncos head coach Sean Payton has utilized Engram in a Joker role in the offense, and Schatz believes that Otton would fit in nicely as a proficient blocker who is good enough in the passing game that he cannot be ignored by opposing defenses.

articles.headline: Five grabs to cap first NFL season
articles.story: Warren caught five of eight targets for 26 yards in Sunday's 38-30 loss to the Texans.
articles.analysis: With  Riley Leonard  getting his first career start at quarterback, Warren wound up being nothing more than a security blanket and check-down option for his fellow rookie, with his longest gain going for just nine yards. The five receptions still led the Colts, however. Warren puts his first NFL season in the books with a 76-817-4 line on 112 targets, and depending on how the team solves its QB dilemma this offseason, the tight end could be poised for a Year 2 breakout.

articles.headline: Second among TEs in YAC
articles.story: Warren finished second among tight ends with 487 yards after the catch, per  Dane Brugler of The Athletic , trailing only Trey McBride's 577 in the 2025 regular season.
articles.analysis: Brugler named Warren to his 2025 All-Rookie team, choosing the 14th overall pick over Harold Fannin of the Browns. Warren faltered down the stretch along with the rest of the Colts' offense as Indianapolis dropped its final seven games following an 8-2 start, but he still led all rookie tight ends with 76 catches and 817 receiving yards while excelling after the catch.

articles.headline: Makes most FGs of career in 2025-26
articles.story: McLaughlin went 3-for-4 on FG tries in Saturday's 16-14 win over Carolina and finished the campaign with a career-best 32 field-goal makes on 38 attempts over 17 contests. He also made 32 of 33 extra-point tries.
articles.analysis: McLaughlin's lone miss Saturday was blocked, but he accounted for the Buccaneers' final nine points with field goals from 29, 36 and 48 yards out, respectively. His leg helped Tampa Bay notch a close victory to keep the team's playoff hopes alive, but the Bucs were ultimately eliminated when Atlanta beat the Saints on Sunday. McLaughlin's 32 field-goal tries and 38 attempts during the campaign were both career-high marks, though this was the first time he converted fewer than 90 percent of his tries since 2022. Nonetheless, the veteran kicker thrived from long distance, going 11-for-12 on FG attempts from 50-plus yards. McLaughlin is signed through next year, so he'll almost certainly be Tampa Bay's kicker when the team begins the regular season next September.

articles.headline: Misses one FG in Week 17 loss
articles.story: McLaughlin made one of two field-goal attempts and both of his extra-point tries in Sunday's 20-17 loss to the Dolphins.

articles.headline: Wins starting job
articles.story: McLaughlin appears set to be the Buccaneers' kicker in 2023 after  Tom Pelissero of NFL Network  reports the team waived Rodrigo Blankenship.
articles.analysis: Both kickers saw action for Indianapolis last season, but McLaughlin won that battle and went on to kick 30 field goals in 16 games for the Colts, and now he's won this one, too. The Bucs could always bring in further competition before Week 1, but assuming McLaughlin's job is secure, he'll be supporting an offense with unsettled situations at quarterback and running back but that still features the talented duo of Mike Evans and Chris Godwin at wide receiver.

articles.headline: Tosses four TDs in playoff loss
articles.story: Love completed 24 of 46 passes for 323 yards with four touchdowns and no interceptions while adding one rush for 11 yards in the Packers' 31-27 wild-card loss to the Bears on Saturday. He also fumbled once but recovered.
articles.analysis: Love was cooking over the first two quarters of Saturday's wild-card matchup, connecting on first-half touchdown passes of seven, 18 and one yard to  Christian Watson ,  Jayden Reed  and  Romeo Doubs , respectively. Following a quiet, scoreless third quarter, Love capped off a six-play, 54-yard drive with a 23-yard scoring toss to  Matthew Golden  that answered a Bears TD just past the midway point of the final period. However, Love couldn't counter either of  Caleb Williams ' two subsequent scoring strikes, although a  Brandon McManus  missed field goal and a pair of late drops didn't do the veteran signal-caller any favors. Love also mishandled the snap on the final play, which seemed to disrupt his timing and led to some frantic scrambling before he threw incomplete into the end zone as
